behavior_summary: This script implements a guard NPC with simple inspect (U8P_GUARD9::look) text, proximity revive/activation behavior, a scheduled respawn/activation routine, and a multi-option conversation handler.
U8P_GUARD9::look: when the player 'looks' at the guard the script barks a one-line description that differs if the NPC is dead or alive.

U8P_GUARD9::enterFastArea: triggered when an actor enters the guard's fast-area. If the NPC is dead it clears dead/combat state and teleports the guard to fixed coordinates (revive/reset). If alive it calls engine activation methods and suspends (activation/AI initialization).

U8P_GUARD9::schedule: writes several U8P_GUARD9::schedule/timing locals, invokes an engine scheduling call, suspends, and if process_result() is true it clears the NPC dead state and calls a follow-up engine method — inferred to manage timed respawn or activation.

U8P_GUARD9::use: full conversation flow—spawns a helper, resets an external ref, tallies several global conditions (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree) into a counter that can trigger panic dialog, chooses greetings based on guard9Met and guard9Pissed (including a random variation), builds a 4-option menu, removes options after selection, and sets flags like guard9Met, guard9Pissed, guard9Thanked depending on choices.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD9::look branches on Npc::isDead(this) to emit either 'dead guardsman' or 'guardsman' bark.

U8P_GUARD9::enterFastArea: if Npc::isDead(this) then call Npc::clrDead,this; Npc::clrInCombat,this; Npc::teleport(...) to revive and place the guard; else run METHOD::func091C, suspend, METHOD::func15E7 and suspend (activation sequence).

U8P_GUARD9::schedule writes multiple scheduling locals, calls METHOD::func143A then suspends; after suspend it checks (word)process_result() and if true calls Npc::clrDead(this) and METHOD::func133F and suspends (finalize respawn).

U8P_GUARD9::use conversation: spawn FREE::func1C19 and call FREE::resetRef early; increment local3 for each of hydrosFree/hasHeart/pyrosFree, if count>1 show panic text and end; otherwise pick greeting by guard9Met/guard9Pissed (urandom used for variation), set guard9Met on first meet, then present a menu of 4 options and handle choice-specific lines and flag writes; MenuRemove prevents repeat choices; ‘Bye’ ends conversation.
open_questions: Exact semantics of METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func133F are unknown — they appear to be engine-level activation/scheduling helpers and their side effects are inferred.
Precise meaning and range of the scheduling local values written in U8P_GUARD9::schedule (locals written to 0x28, 0x30, 0x245F, 0x347F in raw) are not recovered from the script.

Purpose and behavior of FREE::func1C19 (spawned at conversation start) and FREE::resetRef's target are unclear from provided code; they appear to be auxiliary systems invoked during conversation initialization.
Whether teleport coordinates in U8P_GUARD9::enterFastArea place the guard at a permanent patrol point or a temporary safe spot is inferred but not certain.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD9::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description / bark when the player examines the guard, distinguishing dead vs alive.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine 'U8P_GUARD9::look' / examine action on the NPC
Reads NPC state via Npc::isDead(this) to decide which bark to emit

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::look
function_map.role: inspect handler that barks a short description based on NPC dead state

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::enterFastArea
function_map.role: proximity trigger that attempts to revive/reset the NPC or run activation routines when the player/actor approaches

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::schedule
function_map.role: scheduled/timer-driven routine that sets scheduling values, invokes engine U8P_GUARD9::schedule functionality, and on success revives/finalizes NPC activation

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD9::use
function_map.role: conversation handler presenting greetings and a 4-option menu, updating guard-related flags and removing options after U8P_GUARD9::use

internal_calls.purpose: Part of non-dead activation path before suspend; likely an engine activation/initialization call
Continuation/cleanup in activation sequence after suspend
Start/register scheduled action or query timed behavior before suspend
Called after process_result() true to finalize respawn/activation
Spawned async helper invoked at conversation start (exact task unknown)
Reset an external reference/flag early in conversation flow

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the NPC is dead to choose which bark to emit
Decide revive vs activation path when actor enters fast area
Clear NPC dead state as part of revival/reset
Clear NPC in-combat flag when reviving/resetting
Move the NPC to a fixed map/coordinate during revive/reset
Check success of a suspended engine call to decide whether to proceed with revive actions

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Influences conversation branching when answering duty/location questions

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to a counter that can trigger panic dialog if multiple conditions are true

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the same counter and can select a specific greeting branch

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the same counter for panic dialog decision

reads.name: guard9Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to select first-time vs repeated greeting

reads.name: guard9Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to choose irritated greeting if previously angered

writes.name: guard9Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to true on the first conversation to mark the NPC as met

writes.name: guard9Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set/cleared by conversation choices to mark the NPC as angry or calm

writes.name: guard9Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player thanks the guard via a menu option

writes.name: (per-NPC) dead/in-combat state (Npc::clrDead / Npc::clrInCombat)
writes.likely_effect: Clears NPC dead/in-combat flags as part of revival/reset; these are NPC state flags rather than globals

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a guard NPC with simple inspect (U8P_GUARD9::look) text, proximity revive/activation behavior, a scheduled respawn/activation routine, and a multi-option conversation handler.
